_comment: Bundled curated manifest of public-domain Eastern Orthodox icons. Every entry links to a freely-licensed source image. This file ships with the plugin so it works fully offline of any crawler and provides a guaranteed fallback provider.
license_note: All entries are historical works in the public domain (author died 70+ years ago) or released under CC0/PD by the hosting institution. Attribution is preserved for good practice.

icons.name: Christ Pantocrator (Sinai)
icons.description: The 6th-century encaustic icon of Christ Pantocrator from Saint Catherine's Monastery, Mount Sinai.

icons.name: Theotokos of Vladimir
icons.feast: Feast of the Vladimir Icon (June 3, July 6, September 8)
icons.description: The 12th-century Byzantine icon of the Virgin Mary and Child known as Our Lady of Vladimir.

icons.name: The Holy Trinity (Rublev)
icons.description: Andrei Rublev's early 15th-century icon of the Old Testament Trinity (the Hospitality of Abraham).

icons.name: Saint Nicholas of Myra
icons.description: Icon of Saint Nicholas the Wonderworker, Archbishop of Myra in Lycia.

icons.name: Saint George and the Dragon
icons.description: Icon of the Great Martyr George slaying the dragon.

icons.name: The Nativity of Christ
icons.description: Traditional icon of the Nativity of the Lord.
